source_name: MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references
content:
# MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: AI dashboard background, automation console, analytics interface, agent workflow UI, data visualization shell, glass command center
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/requestAnimationFrame

Visual traits:
- low-density data currents moving behind glass panels
- slow particle drift with occasional connection lines
- depth layers of dust, telemetry, and soft light trails
- technical atmosphere without sci-fi clutter
- subtle motion that supports AI or analytics context

Use when:
- AI dashboard background
- automation console
- analytics interface
- agent workflow UI
- data visualization shell
- glass command center

Avoid when:
- high particle density behind text
- cursor-chasing particles in serious productivity tools
- fast network-line animations
- bright blinking nodes
- turning the whole UI into a sci-fi dashboard when the domain is operational
- running canvas animation without reduced-motion or visibility pause

AI summary: Use data current particle backgrounds for AI, analytics, and automation glass interfaces. Keep particles sparse, slow, peripheral, and pauseable; implement with canvas and requestAnimationFrame when many points are needed, with reduced-motion and visibility throttling.

source_name: MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references
content:
# MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: AI dashboard background, automation console, analytics interface, agent workflow UI, data visualization shell, glass command center
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/requestAnimationFrame

- Keep particles sparse enough that empty space remains visible behind glass.
- Use data current motion in peripheral zones and behind overview cards, not under forms or dense text.
- Prefer horizontal or orbital flow paths that imply system activity without distracting from controls.
- Fade particles near modal and navigation surfaces to keep hierarchy clear.
- Use one data-current layer plus one ambient glow layer; avoid stacking many unrelated particle systems.

AI summary: Use data current particle backgrounds for AI, analytics, and automation glass interfaces. Keep particles sparse, slow, peripheral, and pauseable; implement with canvas and requestAnimationFrame when many points are needed, with reduced-motion and visibility throttling.

source_name: MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references
content:
# MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references - Accessibility Notes

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: AI dashboard background, automation console, analytics interface, agent workflow UI, data visualization shell, glass command center
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/requestAnimationFrame

- Provide reduced-motion mode that stops particle movement or uses a static constellation.
- Do not make particle motion a required status indicator; pair loading or activity states with text.
- Keep moving points and lines below text contrast priority.
- Avoid rapid flicker, dense strobing, or pointer-chasing effects near interactive elements.

AI summary: Use data current particle backgrounds for AI, analytics, and automation glass interfaces. Keep particles sparse, slow, peripheral, and pauseable; implement with canvas and requestAnimationFrame when many points are needed, with reduced-motion and visibility throttling.

source_name: MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references
content:
# MDN Canvas animation and requestAnimationFrame references - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: AI dashboard background, automation console, analytics interface, agent workflow UI, data visualization shell, glass command center
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/requestAnimationFrame

- Use canvas for particles when the background needs many moving points; use requestAnimationFrame for synchronized browser painting.
- Scale particle count by viewport area and device capability; keep mobile counts lower.
- Use delta-time based movement so speed is stable across refresh rates.
- Pause or heavily throttle animation when hidden, when reduced-motion is requested, or when the tab loses visibility.
- Draw at a reduced internal resolution and blur/upscale softly if the current is atmospheric rather than precise.
- Use color tokens for particle hue, line alpha, and glow so the scene can match dark or light glass themes.

AI summary: Use data current particle backgrounds for AI, analytics, and automation glass interfaces. Keep particles sparse, slow, peripheral, and pauseable; implement with canvas and requestAnimationFrame when many points are needed, with reduced-motion and visibility throttling.
